title: Divisor
body:
In mathematics, a divisor of an integer 
  
    
      
        n
        ,
      
    
    {\displaystyle n,}
  
 also called a factor of 
  
    
      
        n
        ,
      
    
    {\displaystyle n,}
  
 is an integer 
  
    
      
        m
      
    
    {\displaystyle m}
  
 that may be multiplied by some integer to produce 
  
    
      
        n
        .
      
    
    {\displaystyle n.}
  
 In this case, one also says that 
  
    
      
        n
      
    
    {\displaystyle n}
  
 is a multiple of 
  
    
      
        m
        .
      
    
    {\displaystyle m.}
  
 An integer 
  
    
      
        n
      
    
    {\displaystyle n}
  
 is divisible or evenly divisible by another integer 
  
    
      
        m
      
    
    {\displaystyle m}
  
 if 
  
    
      
        m
      
    
    {\displaystyle m}
  
 is a divisor of 
  
    
      
        n
      
    
    {\displaystyle n}
  
; this implies dividing 
  
    
      
        n
      
    
    {\displaystyle n}
  
 by 
  
    
      
        m
      
    
    {\displaystyle m}
  
 leaves no remainder.
categories: Articles lacking in-text citations from June 2015
